common.department_header: DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING
common.name: Santosh Kumar

targets: Define the problem statement and major objectives of AURA.
Review related work on electronics inventory management, maker workflows, and component retrieval issues.
Draft the high-level architecture for the host, node, inventory, and circuit workspace layers.
Shortlist the initial hardware and software stack required for the prototype.
Prepare the implementation direction and documentation roadmap for the coming weeks.
achievements: Finalized the initial project scope around physical component localization and deterministic circuit assistance.
Completed the early literature review and documented the major pain points in existing workflows.
Prepared the first high-level architecture covering the host, distributed nodes, inventory layer, and workspace.
Shortlisted the initial prototype stack using ESP32, nRF24L01+, WS2812 indication, and a structured software workflow.
Created the first week-wise execution direction for implementation, documentation, and reporting.
future_work: Begin designing the inventory data model and physical storage addressing strategy.
Refine the host-to-node communication flow and physical location mapping logic.
Draft the first UI flow for search, lookup, and component retrieval.
Start planning the deterministic JSON model for circuit representation.
week_summary: Days/Time
5 Days a week Monday to Friday.
Monday
Defined the project problem, scope, and major objectives for AURA.
Tuesday
Reviewed related work on component management, maker workflows, and hardware retrieval challenges.
Wednesday
Sketched the first high-level architecture for host, node, inventory, and circuit workspace modules.
Thursday
Compared hardware options and shortlisted ESP32, nRF24L01+, WS2812, and low-power node control choices.
Friday
Organized reference notes, prepared the execution plan, and completed the first weekly progress documentation.
weeks.research_status: STATUS OF RESEARCH PAPER: Initial literature review started; foundational references on cyber-physical electronics assistance, component inventory workflows, and deterministic circuit representation were identified. Estimated overall project completion by the end of this week: 11%.

targets: Develop the first inventory model covering component type, value, quantity, and physical location.
Study storage addressing strategies for drawers, boxes, and distributed shelves.
Draft the communication flow between the ESP32 host and the distributed locator nodes.
List the primary user actions for search, locate, and inventory checking.
Prepare the initial component metadata structure for later circuit linkage.
achievements: Designed the first version of the inventory record structure for value-aware and quantity-aware tracking.
Mapped the basic physical storage addressing logic for racks, drawers, and grouped component rows.
Prepared the host-to-node communication concept using addressed wireless triggering.
Defined the main user actions for manual search, guided lookup, and part-location confirmation.
Documented the initial metadata fields needed for component identity, value, quantity, and notes.
future_work: Begin drafting the canonical JSON structure for circuit representation.
Refine the digital-to-physical handoff flow from selected parts to locator activation.
Start planning the host-side search interface and minimal control workflow.
Review data needed for linking inventory with future circuit suggestions.

weeks.research_status: STATUS OF RESEARCH PAPER: Literature review expanded with focus on inventory-aware hardware workflows and physical component retrieval systems. Estimated overall project completion by the end of this week: 19%.

targets: Design the canonical JSON structure for deterministic circuit representation.
List supported component metadata fields needed for pins, constraints, and inventory linkage.
Identify the first local calculation and rule-checking cases for supported circuit patterns.
Plan how AI-assisted circuit output will remain structured and reviewable.
Define the first bridge points between inventory records and circuit recommendations.
achievements: Prepared the first draft of the canonical JSON model for structured circuit state.
Defined key metadata fields for pins, package constraints, and inventory association.
Selected initial deterministic checks for simple circuit reasoning and supported-pattern analysis.
Documented the requirement that AI suggestions must remain structured rather than free-form.
Outlined the first connection points between owned inventory and circuit recommendation logic.
future_work: Refine the workspace flow for viewing and editing structured circuit data.
Begin planning the local-first editing model before backend synchronization.
Prepare the first concept for proposal compare and review behavior.
Continue narrowing the supported V1 feature set for deterministic operation.

targets: Start shaping the workspace flow for circuit viewing, editing, and review.

Outlined how local analysis and inventory-linked explanation panels should be presented.

targets: Progress the hardware-side prototype using the ESP32 host and low-power node concept.

achievements: Advanced the host-and-node prototype direction for addressed physical component localization.
Refined the idea that one node can cover dense component rows while multiple nodes cover larger spaces.
Improved the inventory sufficiency logic to include value-specific quantity checks.
Documented the whole-project highlighting workflow for collecting all required parts together.
Clarified the ESP32 host role as the bridge between smartphone interaction and node communication.
